summary: 6 newsletters ingested. Dominant theme: Fable 5 coverage across 3 independent sources (Import AI, AI by Aakash, AINews/Swyx). Secondary: Tencent open frontier model, NVIDIA GPU debt/infrastructure economics. All raw digests contain only redirect URLs (substack/beehiiv) — no inline article text. Downstream wiki-ingest should resolve primary article URLs via open.substack.com direct links where available.

newsletters.subject: Import AI 464: Fables writes GPU kernels; AI automation; and analog computation

wiki_relevance.notes: New capability: AI-generated GPU kernels. Update fable-5.md with kernel-writing capability. Potential new concept page for AI-generated code at the systems/infra level (beyond application code).
Broad topic — need article content to determine specifics. Likely agent/workflow automation developments.
Emerging topic: analog/neuromorphic compute for AI workloads. If substantiated, create concept page under concepts/.

newsletters.subject: 🎙️ How I AI: Sonnet 5 review & How to run autonomous coding agents from your phone

wiki_relevance.notes: Sonnet 5 likely refers to Claude Sonnet 5 (Anthropic). If distinct model from Fable 5/Mythos 5, may need new entity page. Otherwise update existing Claude model hierarchy.
Mobile-first autonomous coding agent workflow. Relevant to coding agent accessibility and remote development patterns.

newsletters.subject: 🐉 Tencent's Open Model Crashes the Frontier

wiki_relevance.notes: Tencent releasing an open model that competes at frontier level. Major event for open-source AI and Chinese AI competitiveness. Update tencent.md and potentially create event page. beehiiv links need resolution — no direct article URL available from digest.

newsletters.subject: Fable 5 is back. Stop Wasting It on Tasks Sonnet Can Do

wiki_relevance.notes: Practical guidance on model selection: when to use Fable 5 vs cheaper Sonnet models. Update fable-5.md with usage best practices / cost-efficiency guidance.

newsletters.subject: Nvidia GPU Debt Backstop Unleashes the AI Project Trinity: Capital, Offtake and Datacenters

wiki_relevance.topic: NVIDIA GPU debt backstop financing model
wiki_relevance.notes: NVIDIA's financial engineering: debt backstop for AI infrastructure projects. The 'Trinity' = capital + offtake + datacenters. Significant for AI infrastructure economics. Update semianalysis.md and relevant infrastructure concept pages.
AINews comprehensive guide to Fable. High signal — swyx's AINews typically includes curated links, technical deep-dives, and community reactions. Core article for Fable 5 wiki page enrichment.

cross_newsletter_theme.description: 3/6 newsletters focus on Fable 5 — capability expansion (GPU kernels), practical usage guides (task allocation), and comprehensive field guides. This signals Fable 5 has moved from initial release (June 2026) to ecosystem adoption phase.
wiki_implications: fable-5.md needs a significant update with multiple new sections
Consider creating a dedicated comparison: fable-5-vs-sonnet-for-tasks
GPU kernel writing may warrant its own concept page under concepts/ai-code-generation/

downstream_notes: All 6 newsletters are redirect-only (substack/beehiiv tracking URLs). The newsletter-wiki-ingest step must resolve primary article URLs to scrape full content.
Primary URLs (open.substack.com direct links) are available for 5/6 newsletters and should be used instead of redirect links.
Beehiiv newsletter (uid=333) has no direct URL — all links are beehiiv tracking redirects. Need alternative resolution.
Total unique articles to resolve: ~118 links, but many are substack infrastructure (like/share/comment/reaction links). Effective unique content articles estimated at ~15-20.
